title: Executive – Youth Division
description:
[Contract Position]

Job Description

This role involves managing operations and administration to ensure the successful delivery of SINDA’s initiatives, supporting Indian women and youth aged 7 to 35 years.

Responsibilities:

  • Ensure the implementation of youth programmes is aligned with the division’s objectives
  • Plan and implement effective and focused programmes to equip youth with resources to be empowered and inspired
  • Serve as one of the liaison officers to coordinate with other organisations, ensure smooth execution of programmes, manage communications and feedback from stakeholders professionally and lead outreach initiatives
  • Ensure timely and efficient update of documentation and data
  • Evaluate and analyse the effectiveness of the programmes in meeting the needs of the clients and community and to submit periodic reports
  • Identify areas of improvements in these programmes, implement quality control and compliance measures and develop information materials for distribution to stakeholders
  • Keep abreast of current developments and trends, and apply domain knowledge to trends within the social sector
  • Ensure adherence to SOPs, Financial and Procurement guidelines
  • Participate in Divisional and SINDA-wide events when required
  • Any other duties as assigned by Reporting Officer / HOD

Requirements:

  • Minimum Degree in any discipline
  • Minimum 1-2 years of working experience in a programme / event related role
  • Meticulous and good problem-solving skills
  • Able to work independently and in a team
  • Demonstrate emotional maturity and compassion when working with youths
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills and ability to manage stakeholders across different levels.
  • Good organisational and time-management skills
  • Proficient in MS PowerPoint and Excel for presentations and reports
  • Able to perform basic data analysis using Excel/Power BI would be an added advantage
  • Flexible to work on regular weekday evenings and Saturdays; Time off will be given to compensate the overtime hours worked

Our Welfare:

We aim to take care of your well-being.

We provide

  • Flexible working hours
  • AWS/Bonuses
  • Health insurance
  • Wellness programmes

Interested candidates are invited to send their resumes with current and expected salary information.

Only shortlisted candidates will be notified. We thank all applicants for their interest.

Please note that your response to this advertisement and communications with us pursuant to this advertisement will constitute informed consent to the collection, use and/or disclosure of personal data by SINDA to carry out its business in compliance with the relevant provisions of the Personal Data Protection Act 2012. Click here to learn more about SINDA’s Data Privacy Policy.

postedCompany.description:
The Singapore Indian Development Association (SINDA) is a self-help group that works to uplift the Singapore Indian Community. SINDA’s key thrusts are in Education, Family Services and Active Collaboration.

SINDA was formed in August 1991 to address the pressing educational and socio-economic issues facing the community. In this light, SINDA was formed to raise the academic level of Indian students. In addition to that primary objective, SINDA also offers a range of programmes and services to help families who approach us for various forms of assistance. We assist with the social and economic development of individuals. Youths too benefit from our programmes that are aimed at mentoring and equipping them with leadership skills.

The core thrust of SINDA is ‘Maximising Educational Opportunities for ALL’. The opportunities are great for every student in Singapore. He/ she and the family must maximise these opportunities.
postedCompany.name: Singapore Indian Development Association (SINDA)
